blocks:
Sūrat al-ʿAlaq

Meccan. Its number of verses is twenty according to the Hijazi count, nineteen according to the Iraqi count, and eighteen according to the Shami count. Its points of difference are two verses: الَّذِي يَنْهى ** (96:9) [is counted] by others besides the Shami count, and لَئِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهِ ** (96:15) [is counted] by the Hijazi count. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ ** (1) Read in the Name of your Lord who created. He created all creatures according to the requirement of His wisdom and brought them forth from non-existence (العدم) into existence (الوجود) by the perfection of His power. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that it is the first Sūrah revealed. He said: Gabriel descended upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, read." He said: "And what should I read?" He said: اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ ** (Read in the Name of your Lord who created), meaning, He created your ancient light before [all] things.

(٢) خَلَقَ الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ عَلَقٍ ** (2) He created man from a clinging mass. From congealed blood after the nutfa (النّطفة). (٣) اقْرَأْ وَرَبُّكَ الْأَكْرَمُ. (3) Read, and your Lord is the Most Generous, (٤) الَّذِي عَلَّمَ بِالْقَلَمِ (4) who taught by the pen, Al-Qummi said: He taught man writing (الكتابة) by which the affairs of the world are completed in the Easts of the earth and its Wests. (٥) عَلَّمَ الْإِنْسانَ ما لَمْ يَعْلَمْ ** (5) taught man what he did not know. From the types of guidance (الهدى) and clarification (البيان). And Al-Qummi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: It means He عَلَّمَ ** (taught) Ali (عليّاً) concerning the writing (الكتابة) for you ** ما لَمْ يَعْلَمْ ** (what he did not know) before that. It was said: He (glory be to Him) enumerated the beginning of man's affair and its end, in manifestation of what He bestowed upon him by transferring him from the lowest ranks to the highest, in confirmation of His Lordship (ربوبيّته) and realization of His Most Generous nature (أكرميّته).

(٦) كَلَّا ** is a reprimand (ردع) for the one who disbelieved in Allah's favor due to his transgression. إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَيَطْغى. (6) No indeed! man is indeed rebellious, (٧) أَنْ رَآهُ اسْتَغْنى ** (7) when he sees himself sufficiently rich. Meaning, he saw himself as self-sufficient (مستغنية). Al-Qummi said: إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ ** (Indeed, man) when اسْتَغْنى ** (he sees himself sufficiently rich), he disbelieves, transgresses, and denies the return (الرجعى) to his Lord. (٨) إِنَّ إِلى رَبِّكَ الرُّجْعى ** (8) Indeed, to your Lord is the return. The address is directed to man, using a shift in address (الالتفات), as a threat and a warning against the consequence of transgression. (٩) أَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي يَنْهى. (9) Have you seen him who forbids (١٠) عَبْداً إِذا صَلَّى ** (10) a servant when he prays? What will be his recompense and what will be his state? Al-Qummi said: Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah used to forbid people from prayer and from obeying Allah and His Messenger. So He said: أَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي يَنْهى عَبْداً إِذا صَلَّى (Have you seen him who forbids a servant when he prays?) And in al-Majma', it is narrated in the Hadith: Indeed, Abu Jahl said: "Does Muhammad rub his face in the dust among you?" They said: "Yes." He said: "By the One he swears by, if I see him doing that, I will surely step on his neck." It was said to him: "There he is, praying." So he set off to step on his neck, but he did not return to them except retreating on his heels and warding off with his hands. They asked: "What is wrong with you, O Abu al-Hakam?" He said: "Indeed, between me and him is a trench of fire, and he is [surrounded by] wings." And the Prophet of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By the One in Whose hand is my soul, had he approached me, the angels would have snatched him limb by limb." So Allah, the Glorious, revealed أَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي يَنْهى (Have you seen him who forbids) until the end of the Surah.

**(١١) ** أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ كانَ عَلَى الْهُدى ** (11) Tell me, if he is on [true] guidance, meaning the servant who is forbidden from prayer, and He is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(١٢) ** أَوْ أَمَرَ بِالتَّقْوى ** (12) or bids to Godwariness, [bidding] away from polytheism (الشرك), meaning He commanded sincerity (الإخلاص), monotheism (التوحيد), and the fear of Allah, the Exalted. What would be the state of the one who forbids Him from prayer and restrains Him from it?

(١٣) ** أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَذَّبَ ** (13) Tell me, if he denies and turns away, [meaning] the one who forbids Him [denies] وَتَوَلَّى ** (and turns away) from faith (الإيمان), and turns away from accepting it and listening to it—what punishment does he deserve for that?

**(١٤) ** أَلَمْ يَعْلَمْ بِأَنَّ اللهَ يَرى ** (14) does he not know that Allah sees? what he does and knows what he performs.

(١٥) ** كَلَّا ** (15) No indeed! If he does not desist, We will surely drag him by the forelock— A deterrent (ردع) for the prohibitor. لَئِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهِ ** (If he does not desist) from what he is engaged in, لَنَسْفَعاً بِالنَّاصِيَةِ (We will surely drag him by the forelock)—We will surely seize him by his forelock and drag him by it to the Fire. السفع (السفع) is seizing something and pulling it severely.

(١٦) ** ناصِيَةٍ كاذِبَةٍ خاطِئَةٍ. (16) a lying, sinful forelock.

(١٧) ** فَلْيَدْعُ نادِيَهُ ** (17) Let him call his gang! meaning the people of his club (ناديه) to help him. This is the assembly (المجلس) where the people gather (يَنْتَدِي). It is narrated that Abu Jahl passed by the Messenger of Allah while He was praying and said: "Did I not forbid you?" So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم spoke harshly to him. Abu Jahl said: "Are you threatening me, when I have the most numerous club (نادياً) in the valley?" So [the Ayah] was revealed. And al-Qummi said: When Abu Talib died, Abu Jahl and al-Walid (may the curses of Allah be upon them both) called out: "Come, kill Muhammad, for the one who supported Him has died!" So Allah said: فَلْيَدْعُ نادِيَهُ.** (Let him call his gang!)

(١٨) سَنَدْعُ الزَّبانِيَةَ ** (We will summon the Zabaaniyah) so that they may drag him to the Fire. And they are originally the police/guards (الشرط), the singular of which is زَبْنِيَة. Al-Qummi said: Just as he called for the killing of Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, We, too, summon الزَّبانِيَةَ. (the Zabaaniyah). (١٩) كَلَّا ** (No indeed!) It is also a deterrent for the prohibitor (the one who forbids). لا تُطِعْهُ ** (do not obey him) And you, remain firm upon the worship of your Lord, وَاسْجُدْ (and prostrate), and persist in your prostration, وَاقْتَرِبْ ** (and draw near), and draw near to your Lord.

In al-Kafi and al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The closest the servant is to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is when he is prostrating. And that is His saying, the Almighty: وَاسْجُدْ وَاقْتَرِبْ. (and prostrate, and draw near.)

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: that which is similar in meaning.

In al-Khisal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, the obligatory prostrations (العزائم) are four: اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ (Read in the Name of your Lord who created), وَالنَّجْمِ (By the Star), تَنْزِيلُ السَّجْدَةِ (The Revelation of the Prostration), and حم السَّجْدَةِ (Ha Mim, The Prostration).

And it is added in al-Majma': And what is besides them in the entire Quran is recommended (مسنون) and not obligatory (مفروض).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida, from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them): Indeed, the first Surah revealed was بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful. Read in the Name of your Lord), and the last Surah revealed was إِذا جاءَ نَصْرُ اللهِ (When the help of Allah comes).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and similarly in Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever recites اقْرَأْ بِاسْمِ رَبِّكَ (Read in the Name of your Lord) during his day or night, and then dies during that day or night, he dies a martyr (شهيداً), and Allah will resurrect him as a martyr, or he will be like one who struck with his sword in the way of Allah alongside the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله.
